title: Panopticon Event
description: The canonical shape of a single event in the Panopticon event log. Every collector — Android Accessibility, NotificationListener, UsageStats, desktop, manual journal/checkin, instrument response, cognitive test, purchase, browse, health, and coach briefing — emits events conforming to this schema. Stored in the local SQLite `events` table. The payload_json shape varies by (source, event_type); canonical shapes are documented in $defs and in docs/data-sources.md, docs/journal-pipeline.md, and docs/daily-briefing.md.

id.description: Local autoincrement primary key. Assigned by SQLite; not present in events emitted by collectors before insert.

timestamp_utc.description: Epoch milliseconds, UTC. Always UTC — store local-time interpretation via timezone_offset.

timezone_offset.description: Minutes offset from UTC at the moment the event was captured. e.g. -480 for PST. Stored alongside the timestamp because the user may travel and a single 'local time' field would lose information.

source.description: The collector or subsystem that produced this event.

event_type.description: Event subtype, scoped per source. See $defs._event_type_enums and the docs/ folder for the canonical list of (source, event_type) pairs and their payload shapes.

package_name.description: Android package name for app-related events (e.g. 'com.instagram.android'). Null for events without an associated app.

payload_json.description: Structured event-specific data. Shape depends on (source, event_type). Stored as a JSON string in SQLite; modeled as an object here. See $defs for canonical payload shapes per event type.

schema_version.description: Schema version of this event payload. Incremented when the shape of payload_json for any (source, event_type) changes in a non-backwards-compatible way.

_event_type_enums.description: Reference: valid event_type values per source. Not enforced at the schema level (since payload shapes vary), but documented here for collectors and forkers.

manual_journal_entry_payload.description: Free-form prose journal for a given day, optionally segmented by section after pipeline processing. The pipeline extracts entities, events, sentiment, and self-hypotheses from the raw text and writes those into sections[] and self_hypotheses[]. The original prose is preserved verbatim in `text`.

text.description: The full original prose as the user wrote it. Never modified after capture.

completion_seconds.description: Wall-clock time spent composing, if measurable.

sections.description: Pipeline-extracted segmentation of the prose. Each section tags a slice of text with a category for later querying ('show me all my food sections from the last 90 days').

self_hypotheses.description: First-class testable claims the user made about themselves in the prose. The coach pipeline tests each against the event log and writes test results into coach.hypothesis_test events.

claim.description: Verbatim or near-verbatim quote of the self-statement.

scope_window.description: Time window the claim implicitly covers, e.g. 'always', 'this week', 'since starting X'.

supporting_evidence_query.description: The data query that would test this claim (e.g. event-log filter).

scales_snapshot.description: Optional embedded daily_checkin scales captured alongside the journal entry, for convenience when the user fills both at once. Equivalent to a separate manual.daily_checkin event.

manual_daily_checkin_payload.description: Lightweight sliding-scale self-report. Designed to be filled in 30-60 seconds. Each scale_id is defined in instruments/daily_scales.json.

skipped.description: If the user explicitly skipped this scale today; the skip itself is a signal.

manual_observed_interaction_payload.description: A discrete self-observed interaction the user wants to log explicitly (e.g. 'snapped at dog', 'helped a stranger with directions'). Distinct from the journal because it gets timestamped at the moment, not retrospectively at end-of-day.

parties.description: Free-form labels; the user defines what 'mom' or 'Cooper' resolves to.

manual_notable_event_payload.description: A discrete event in the user's life worth noting (a milestone, a loss, a change of circumstance, a conflict, a meaningful interaction). Backdated when needed — `timestamp_utc` is the event's actual occurrence time, not the log time. Free-form by design; categorization is optional and user-controlled.

category.description: Optional user-defined or suggested category, e.g. 'milestone', 'loss', 'change', 'conflict', 'win', 'medical'.

logged_at_utc.description: Epoch millis at which the user actually entered this event (distinct from `timestamp_utc`, which is when the event itself occurred). Useful for audit when entries are backdated.

instrument_response_payload.description: A complete administration of a validated instrument (PHQ-9, GAD-7, IRI, MFQ, etc.). Item-level responses are stored, NOT just totals — re-scoring with a different algorithm later is then possible without re-administering.

instrument_id.description: Matches a file in instruments/, e.g. 'phq9', 'iri', 'mfq30'.

rt_ms.description: Response time per item; useful for detecting careless responding or rumination on a particular item.

computed_subscales.description: Subscale scores computed at administration time, per the instrument's published scoring algorithm. Cached for convenience; re-derivable from item_responses + the instrument spec.

cognitive_test_result_payload.description: Result of a built-in cognitive test (N-back, simple-RT, Stroop, Raven-style matrices). raw_data preserves trial-level data so re-analysis is possible.

raw_data.description: Per-trial data. Shape depends on test_type.

condition_tags.description: Optional context the user or app tags onto the run, e.g. 'morning', 'post_coffee', 'after_workout', 'tired'. Lets the user later split scores by condition.

purchase_transaction_payload.description: A single purchase, however ingested (manual entry, bank/card import, receipt scan). Categorization supports later analysis of spending patterns, donations, food/alcohol/etc.

browse_search_query_payload.description: A search query the user issued (Google, DuckDuckGo, YouTube, Reddit, etc.). Captured via desktop browser-history collector or manual import. Topic categorization feeds the 'topics of recurring concern' analysis.

browse_page_visit_payload.description: A page visit captured from desktop browser history. Dwell and scroll metrics support reading-depth and topic-breadth subscales.

coach_briefing_payload.description: The daily AI briefing on the home screen. Stored as a first-class event so it's queryable later — 'show me every time the coach flagged my sleep over the last six months'.

date.description: ISO date this briefing is about (typically the day just past).

flags.description: Structured flags surfaced inside the briefing — for later querying without parsing prose. Suicidality, withdrawal, scale-crash, etc.

open_questions.description: Things the system can't tell on its own and wants the user to address in tomorrow's journal.

coach_flag_payload.description: A standalone flag emitted outside the daily briefing context (e.g. by a real-time pipeline noticing a journal entry mentioned suicidality). Same shape as flags inside coach.briefing.

coach_hypothesis_test_payload.description: Result of testing a self-hypothesis (extracted from a journal_entry) against the event log. e.g. user said 'I always eat junk after a run' — this event records: tested over the last 90 days, 11 of 14 post-run windows showed fast-food/sugar logs, status=supported.

media_video_view_payload.description: A video view (e.g. a YouTube video). Sourced from the platform's data export, the desktop browser, or — Phase 6+ — in-app accessibility text capture.

video_id.description: Platform-internal id when available (e.g. YouTube watch?v=ID).

media_audio_play_payload.description: A music/podcast track listen (e.g. Spotify, Apple Music). Sourced from the platform export.

accessibility_text_capture_payload.description: A throttled snapshot of visible text from an app the user has explicitly added to the text-capture allowlist. Captures captions, creator handles, hashtags, visible counts — whatever the screen is rendering at that moment. Off by default; controlled per-package by the user. Never captures input fields, password fields, or content-description-marked sensitive views.

screen_signature.description: Stable hash of the screen's view hierarchy — used to dedupe near-identical scroll positions.

blocks.description: Captured text blocks in render order.

view_id.description: Resource id of the source View, if exposed.
